title: T-Deck: implement trackball input driver (I2C or GPIO quadrature)
description: Driver for T-Deck trackball. Likely I2C based per Renode. Provide input events for movement and buttons. Integrate with Zephyr input subsystem. Ensure no blocking in hot paths per C coding guidelines.
notes: HUMAN: Exact T-Deck UI integration approach - keyboard via I2C (addr 0x55 lilygo_tdeck_keyboard driver), trackball via GPIO quadrature encoder or dedicated I2C driver in lichen/drivers/input/, LVGL for UI (Zephyr lvgl integration + input events), input subsystem events (no direct polling in app), strict no-blocking in hot paths (workqueues, k_poll, async I2C transfers), board overlays/conf: t_deck.overlay + t_deck.conf for both puck and gateway apps (separate Kconfigs), trackball driver modeled on existing Zephyr input drivers (gpio-keys, adc). Leave open pending human input on pinout/quadrature vs I2C. Do not implement yet.
